---
title: "S4A Phase 1: pooling analysis"
author: "Tushar Garg"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
---

```{r setup, include=F}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo=F,message=F, warning=F)
suppressMessages(if (!require("pacman")) install.packages("pacman"))
pacman::p_load(tidyverse,rio,janitor,here,gt)
pacman::p_load(binGroup2)
source(here::here("Scripts","0.functions.R"))
```

```{r import, include=F}
master <- readRDS(here("Outputs","Intermediate outputs","master_clean.rds"))

master_pool <- import(here("Data",folder_name,"pooled_xpert_ultra.csv")) %>% 
  rename(poolsize = no_samp_in_pool)

master_ind_xpert_local <- import(here("Data",folder_name,"xpert_result_local_lab.csv"))

master_ind_xpert_ref <- import(here("Data",folder_name,"xpert_result_reference_lab.csv"))

eff_perf <- readRDS(here("Outputs","Intermediate outputs","efficiency_sen_spec.rds"))

```

```{r pool composition}
pool_composition <- master %>% 
  group_by(poolno, settingACF) %>% 
  summarise(n=n()) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(poolno)) %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from = settingACF, values_from = n) %>% 
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(n_pop = sum(!is.na(c_across(c(PHC, ACF, District, Children))))) %>%
  ungroup()

# creating new column indicating pop composition for pool
pool_composition <- pool_composition %>%
   mutate(
    poolpop = pmap_chr(list(PHC, ACF, District, Children), \(p, a, d, c) {
      parts <- c(
        if (!is.na(p) && p > 0) "PHC",
        if (!is.na(a) && a > 0) "ACF",
        if (!is.na(d) && d > 0) "District",
        if (!is.na(c) && c > 0) "Children"
      )
      if (length(parts) == 0) NA_character_ else paste(parts, collapse = "+")
    })
  )
```

```{r pool summary population}
# summary of pools based on population (settingACF)
pool_summary_pop <- master %>% 
  group_by(poolno, Country_Name, poolsize) %>%
  filter(!is.na(poolno)) %>% 
  summarise(n=n()) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  left_join(.,pool_composition,by="poolno")

# unique(pool_summary_pop$poolpop)

# exporting for viz_sankey_pool_population.R sankey diagram
saveRDS(pool_summary_pop, here("Outputs","Intermediate outputs","pool_summary_pop.rds"))
```

```{r xpert recode}
lvl_xpert <- c("pos","trace","neg","invalid","error","noresult")

# Recode xpert
master <- recode_xpert_pool_results(master,
                                    xpert_result_col = "Xpert_result",
                                    pool_result_col  = "pool_resultt",
                                    xpert_trace = c("as_pos"),
                                    pool_trace  = c("as_pos_xpert"))


# Make it coherent with STARD by removing all invalid pools
# remove all individuals with pooling = NA
master <- master %>% 
  filter(!is.na(pooling))

# check
# master %>% tabyl(pool_xpert_result, pooling)

# Recode pooled xpert values
master_pool <- master_pool %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      all_of(c("xpert_p1_mtb", "xpert_p2_mtb")),
      ~ case_when(
        as.integer(.) == 1 ~ "pos",
        as.integer(.) == 2 ~ "neg",
        as.integer(.) == 3 ~ "trace",
        as.integer(.) == 4 ~ "invalid",
        as.integer(.) == 5 ~ "error",
        as.integer(.) == 6 ~ "noresult",
        TRUE               ~ NA_character_
      )
    )
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    across(all_of(c("xpert_p1_mtb", "xpert_p2_mtb")), ~ factor(., levels = lvl_xpert))
  ) %>% 
  mutate(Country_Name = if_else(Country_Name %in% c("Nigeria JHF", "Nigeria ZRC"), "Nigeria", Country_Name))

# create a final pooled result based on repeated pooled result = xpert_p_mtb
master_pool <- master_pool %>%
  mutate(xpert_p_mtb = case_when(
      # If P2 is NA, keep P1
      is.na(xpert_p2_mtb) ~ as.character(xpert_p1_mtb),
      # If P2 is (pos/trace), prefer P2 over P1
      as.character(xpert_p2_mtb) %in% c("pos","trace") ~ as.character(xpert_p2_mtb),
      # If P1 is inconclusive (invalid/error/noresult), fall back to P2
      as.character(xpert_p1_mtb) %in% c("invalid","error","noresult") ~ as.character(xpert_p2_mtb),
      # If P1 is pos/trace but P2 is not, keep P1 (protect P1 positives)
      as.character(xpert_p1_mtb) %in% c("pos","trace") & !as.character(xpert_p2_mtb) %in% c("pos","trace") ~ as.character(xpert_p1_mtb),
      # Otherwise default to P1 (e.g., both neg, or P2 non-informative)
      TRUE ~ as.character(xpert_p1_mtb)
    ) |> factor(levels = lvl_xpert)
  ) %>% 
  mutate(
    cartridge_used = case_when(
      !is.na(xpert_p1_mtb) & !is.na(xpert_p2_mtb) ~ 2,
      is.na(xpert_p1_mtb) | is.na(xpert_p2_mtb) ~ 1,
    )
  )
```

```{r pool summary}
# summary of pools and repeat pools
pool_summary <- master_pool %>% 
  group_by(Country_Name, poolsize, xpert_p1_mtb, xpert_p2_mtb, xpert_p_mtb) %>%
  summarise(n=n(),
            cartridge_used = sum(cartridge_used)) %>% 
  ungroup()

# exporting to intermediate outputs for Sankey diagram
saveRDS(pool_summary, here("Outputs","Intermediate outputs","pool_summary.rds"))
```

```{r summary of pooled specimen test}
# creating temporary dataframe only for this chunk - during manuscript writing
master_pool2 <- master_pool %>%
  mutate(.row_id = row_number()) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("xpert_p_no_"),
    names_to = "slot",
    values_to = "regno",
    values_drop_na = FALSE
  ) %>%
  left_join(master %>% select(regno, culture), by = "regno") %>%
  mutate(slot = str_extract(slot, "\\d+")) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    id_cols = .row_id,
    names_from = slot,
    values_from = culture,
    names_prefix = "culture_"
  ) %>%
  right_join(master_pool %>% mutate(.row_id = row_number()), by = ".row_id") %>%
  relocate(starts_with("culture_"), .after = xpert_p_no_4) %>%
  mutate(
    pool_culture_any_pos = case_when(
      if_all(starts_with("culture_"), is.na) ~ NA,
      if_any(starts_with("culture_"), ~ .x %in% c("pos","positive","POS","Positive")) ~ TRUE,
      TRUE ~ FALSE)) %>% 
  select(-.row_id)

# False positive (FP) specimen pool result: Xpert MTB positive but culture any positive == FALSE
# (FALSE = All specimen in pool are culture neg; TRUE = at least one positive culture; NA = no culture on either specimen)
master_pool2 %>% tabyl(xpert_p_mtb,pool_culture_any_pos)

```


```{r individual xpert inconclusive proportion}
# What is the proportion of Xpert that need to be repeated because of Invalid/Error/No Result?
master_ind_xpert1 <- bind_rows(
  master_ind_xpert_local %>%
    mutate(source = "local", code = as.integer(xpert_s1_mtb), .keep = "none"),
  master_ind_xpert_ref %>%
    filter(!is.na(xpert_rs_repeated)) %>%
    mutate(source = "ref", code = as.integer(xpert_rs1_mtb), .keep = "none")
) %>%
  filter(!is.na(code)) %>%
  mutate(result = case_when(
    code == 1 ~ "pos",
    code == 2 ~ "neg",
    code == 3 ~ "trace",
    code == 4 ~ "invalid",
    code == 5 ~ "error",
    code == 6 ~ "noresult"
  ))

master_ind_xpert1_summary <- master_ind_xpert1 %>% tabyl(result)

# proportion of codes 4/5/6 out of total 
p_xpert_repeat <- master_ind_xpert1 %>%
  summarise(prop_456 = mean(code %in% c(4,5,6), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  dplyr::pull(prop_456)
```


```{r efficiency analysis}
# n_pools_result = number of pools with result
# n_pools_mtb    = number of pools with MTB detected

# n_pool_cartridges = number of Ultra cartridges used in testing of pools (includes actual number for repeat of inconclusive)
# n_pooling_cartridges = number of Ultra cartridges used in pooling algorithm 
                    # = n_pool_cartridges + (PositivePools*Poolsize)(1+individual repeat testing proportion calculated from actual individual testing data)

# n_ind_result = number of individuals with individual Xpert result
# n_ind_mtb = number of individuals with individual Xpert MTB detected

# n_pooling_result = number of individuals with pooling algorithm result
# n_pooling_mtb = number of individuals with pooling algorithm MTB detected

# test_per_person = number of cartridges used in pooling algorithm per individual with result
# tests_needed_ind = number of cartridge needed for individual testing (inflates for repeat testing as above)

summarize_pooling_efficiency <- function(data_pool,
                                         data_ind,
                                         xpert_repeat_prop = 0,
                                         group_var = NULL,   # e.g. "Country_Name" or NULL
                                         by_poolsize = TRUE) {
  
  # --- helpers ---
  gv   <- if (is.null(group_var)) character(0) else group_var
  by_k <- c(gv, "poolsize")
  join_keys <- c(if (by_poolsize) "poolsize", gv)
  
  # --- pool-level (by poolsize first) ---
  pool_by_k <- data_pool %>%
    mutate(
      has_result = .data$xpert_p_mtb %in% c("pos","trace","neg","invalid","error","noresult"),
      mtb_pos    = .data$xpert_p_mtb %in% c("pos","trace")
    ) %>%
    group_by(across(all_of(by_k))) %>%
    summarise(
      n_pools_result   = n_distinct(.data$poolno[.data$has_result]),
      n_pools_mtb      = n_distinct(.data$poolno[.data$mtb_pos]),
      n_pool_cartridges = sum(.data$cartridge_used, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  
  pool_sum <- if (by_poolsize) {
    pool_by_k %>%
      mutate(n_pooling_cartridges =
               .data$n_pool_cartridges +
               ceiling((.data$n_pools_mtb*.data$poolsize)*(1 + xpert_repeat_prop)))
  } else {
    pool_by_k %>%
      group_by(across(all_of(gv))) %>%
      summarise(
        n_pools_result    = sum(.data$n_pools_result, na.rm = TRUE),
        n_pools_mtb       = sum(.data$n_pools_mtb, na.rm = TRUE),
        n_pool_cartridges = sum(.data$n_pool_cartridges, na.rm = TRUE),
        sum_mtb_k         = sum(.data$n_pools_mtb * .data$poolsize, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop"
      ) %>%
      mutate(n_pooling_cartridges =
               .data$n_pool_cartridges +
               ceiling(.data$sum_mtb_k * (1 + xpert_repeat_prop))) %>%
      select(-.data$sum_mtb_k)
  }
  
  # --- individual-level (by poolsize first) ---
  ind_by_k <- data_ind %>%
    group_by(across(all_of(by_k))) %>%
    summarise(
      n_ind_result     = sum(!is.na(.data$`Xpert Resultt`)),
      n_ind_mtb        = sum(.data$`Xpert Resultt` %in% c("Detected","Trace"), na.rm = TRUE),
      n_pooling_result = sum(!is.na(.data$pooling)),
      n_pooling_mtb    = sum(.data$pooling %in% c("pos"), na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    # mutate(p_positivity = .data$n_pooling_mtb / .data$n_ind_result)
     mutate(p_positivity = .data$n_ind_mtb / .data$n_ind_result) #use individual Xpert positivity

  ind_sum <- if (by_poolsize) {
    ind_by_k
  } else {
    ind_by_k %>%
      group_by(across(all_of(gv))) %>%
      summarise(
        n_ind_result     = sum(.data$n_ind_result, na.rm = TRUE),
        n_ind_mtb        = sum(.data$n_ind_mtb, na.rm = TRUE),
        n_pooling_result = sum(.data$n_pooling_result, na.rm = TRUE),
        n_pooling_mtb    = sum(.data$n_pooling_mtb, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop"
      ) %>%
      # mutate(p_positivity = .data$n_pooling_mtb / .data$n_ind_result)
      mutate(p_positivity = .data$n_ind_mtb / .data$n_ind_result) #use individual Xpert positivity
  }
  
  # --- combine + overall ---
  out <- full_join(pool_sum, ind_sum, by = join_keys)
  
  if (length(join_keys)) {
    out <- out %>% arrange(across(all_of(join_keys)))
  }
  
  out %>%
    mutate(
      test_per_person = n_pooling_cartridges / n_pooling_result,
      tests_needed_ind = ceiling(n_ind_result * (1 + xpert_repeat_prop))
    )
}

# by poolsize only
summary_efficiency <- summarize_pooling_efficiency(master_pool, master, xpert_repeat_prop = p_xpert_repeat)
```

```{r Dorfman calculation using binGroup2}
pool_mat <- function(k) rbind(rep(1, k), seq_len(k))

dorfman_summary <- function(p, k, SeP, SeI, SpP, SpI) {
  oc <- binGroup2::opChar1(
    algorithm   = "D2",
    p           = p,
    Se          = c(SeP, SeI),
    Sp          = c(SpP, SpI),
    hier.config = pool_mat(k),
    print.time  = FALSE
  )
  
  acc_tbl <- binGroup2::Accuracy(oc, individual = FALSE)[["Overall"]] %>% 
    as_tibble()
  
  et_tbl <- binGroup2::ExpTests(oc) 
  
  bind_cols(
    tibble(
      `Input: prevalence (p)`  = p,
      `Input: pool size (k)`   = k,
      `Input: SeP`             = SeP,
      `Input: SeI`             = SeI,
      `Input: SpP`             = SpP,
      `Input: SpI`             = SpI
    ),
    acc_tbl,
    as_tibble(et_tbl)
  )
}

# lookups for sensitivity and specificity based on imported eff_perf
# pool_params <- eff_perf %>%
#   filter(test == "pooling", !is.na(poolsize)) %>%
#   transmute(poolsize = as.integer(poolsize),
#             SeP = sensitivity,
#             SpP = specificity)

xpert_params <- eff_perf %>%
  filter(test == "xpert") %>%
  summarise(SeI = first(sensitivity), SpI = first(specificity))
```


```{r table efficiency}
eff_pool <- summary_efficiency %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(res = list(dorfman_summary(
    p    = p_positivity,
    k    = poolsize,
    SeP  = xpert_params$SeI,
    SeI  = xpert_params$SeI,
    SpP  = xpert_params$SpI,
    SpI  = xpert_params$SpI
  ))) %>%
  unnest(res)

eff_overall <- eff_pool %>%
  summarise(
    poolsize = "Overall",
    across(starts_with("n_"), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE))
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    test_per_person   = n_pooling_cartridges / n_pooling_result,
    tests_needed_ind  = ceiling(n_ind_result * (1 + p_xpert_repeat))
  )

eff_all <- bind_rows(eff_overall, 
                     eff_pool %>% mutate(poolsize = as.character(poolsize))) %>% 
  select(1:p_positivity,ExpTestsPerIndividual,PercentReductionTests
         #,PercentIncreaseTestCap
         )
```

```{r efficiency gt table formatting}
# --- types (ensure numeric) ---
eff_all <- eff_all %>%
  mutate(
    ExpTestsPerIndividual  = parse_number(as.character(ExpTestsPerIndividual)),
    test_per_person        = parse_number(as.character(test_per_person)),
    PercentReductionTestsActual  = parse_number(as.character(1-(n_pooling_cartridges/tests_needed_ind))),
    PercentReductionTests  = parse_number(as.character(PercentReductionTests))/100,
    # PercentIncreaseTestCap = parse_number(as.character(PercentIncreaseTestCap))/100,
    p_positivity           = parse_number(as.character(p_positivity))
  ) %>% 
  select(-n_pooling_result)

label_map <- tribble(
  ~var,                      ~label,                                                                             ~group,
  
  # --- Pool counts ---
  "n_pools_result",           "Pools with result",                                                                "Pool counts",
  "n_pools_mtb",              "Pools with MTB detected",                                                          "Pool counts",
  
  # --- Cartridge utilization ---
  # "n_pool_cartridges",        "Xpert cartridges for pools \u2021",                                               "Xpert cartridge utilization",
  "n_pooling_cartridges",     "Total Xpert cartridges for pooling patient result [*a*] \u2020",                  "Xpert cartridge utilization",
  "tests_needed_ind",         "Total Xpert cartridges for individual testing [*b*] \u2020",                      "Xpert cartridge utilization",
  
  # --- Results ---
  "n_ind_result",             "People with valid result on individual and pooled test [*c*]",                     "Results",
  "n_ind_mtb",                "Number of MTB detected on individual Xpert [*d*]",                                 "Results",
  "n_pooling_mtb",            "Number of MTB detected on pooling patient result",                                 "Results",
  
  # --- Efficiency of pooled testing ---
  "test_per_person",          "Cartridges per valid pooling patient result [*a*/*c*]",                       "Efficiency of pooled testing",
  "PercentReductionTestsActual", "Observed reduction in cartridge use [*e* = 1 – *a*/*b*]",                     "Efficiency of pooled testing",
  "p_positivity",             "Proportion of MTB detected on individual Xpert test [*f* = *d*/*c*]",    "Efficiency of pooled testing",
  "ExpTestsPerIndividual",    "Predicted cartridges per valid pooling patient result \u00A7",     "Efficiency of pooled testing",
  "PercentReductionTests",    "Predicted reduction in cartridges \u00A7",                         "Efficiency of pooled testing"
)

 # mutate(p_positivity = .data$n_pooling_mtb / .data$n_ind_result)
# --- order poolsize and transpose ---
eff_all <- eff_all %>%
  mutate(poolsize = factor(poolsize, levels = c("Overall","2","3","4"))) %>%
  arrange(poolsize)

tab_wide <- eff_all %>%
  select(poolsize, all_of(label_map$var)) %>%
  pivot_longer(-poolsize, names_to = "var", values_to = "value") %>%
  left_join(label_map, by = "var") %>%
  mutate(pool_col = as.character(poolsize)) %>%
  select(group, label, pool_col, value) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = pool_col, values_from = value) %>%
  arrange(factor(group, levels = c("Pool counts","Xpert cartridge utilization","Results","Efficiency of pooled testing"))) %>%
  relocate(group, label)

# --- row selectors for formatting ---
int_labels  <- label_map %>% filter(var %in% c(
  "n_pools_result","n_pools_mtb","n_pool_cartridges","n_pooling_cartridges",
  "n_ind_result","n_ind_mtb","n_pooling_result","n_pooling_mtb","tests_needed_ind"
)) %>% pull(label)

rate_labels <- label_map %>% filter(var %in% c(
  "test_per_person","ExpTestsPerIndividual","p_positivity"
)) %>% pull(label)

pct_labels  <- label_map %>% filter(var %in% c(
  "PercentReductionTests","PercentIncreaseTestCap","PercentReductionTestsActual"
)) %>% pull(label)

# --- build gt ---
efficiency_tbl <- tab_wide %>%
  gt(rowname_col = "label", groupname_col = "group") %>%
  tab_header(
    title = md("**Efficiency of pooled testing**"),
    subtitle = NULL
  ) %>%
  cols_label(`Overall` = "Overall", `2` = "2", `3` = "3", `4` = "4") %>%
  fmt_missing(columns = everything(), missing_text = "\u2014") %>%
  fmt_integer(
    columns = c(Overall, `2`, `3`, `4`),
    rows = label %in% int_labels,
    use_seps = TRUE
  ) %>%
  fmt_number(
    columns = c(Overall, `2`, `3`, `4`),
    rows = label %in% rate_labels,
    decimals = 2
  ) %>%
  fmt_percent(
    columns = c(Overall, `2`, `3`, `4`),
    rows = label %in% pct_labels,
    decimals = 1
  ) %>%
  row_group_order(c("Pool counts","Xpert cartridge utilization","Results","Efficiency of pooled testing")) %>%
  opt_table_outline() %>%
  tab_options(
    table.font.size = px(12),
    data_row.padding = px(4),
    heading.title.font.weight = "bold",
    heading.subtitle.font.size = px(11)
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    md("*Total Xpert cartridges for pooling patient results (*a*) include cartridges used for both pooled tests and follow-on individual testing of positive pools, whereas total Xpert cartridges for individual testing (*b*) represent cartridges used to obtain results for all specimens individually.*")
  ) %>% 
  tab_source_note(
    md(sprintf(
      "\u2020 *Cartridge counts (pooled and individual) include repeats for inconclusive results at an observed rate of %.1f%%.*",
      p_xpert_repeat * 100
    ))
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    md(sprintf(
      "*Cartridges used in testing of pools (%.1f%%) include observed repeats for inconclusive results.*",
      eff_overall$n_pool_cartridges
    ))
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    md(sprintf(
      "\u00A7 *Predicted using Dorfman efficiency for two-stage hierarchical testing based on proportion positive (*d*), pool size, observed sensitivity (%.1f%%), and specificity (%.1f%%).*",
      xpert_params$SeI * 100,
      xpert_params$SpI * 100
    ))
  ) %>% 
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_row_groups(groups = everything())
  ) %>% 
  gt::fmt_markdown(columns = stub())

efficiency_tbl %>% gtsave(here("Outputs","TableEfficiency","Table_efficiency_overall.docx"))
```

```{r}
# At pool level, taking actual retested cartridge.
# At individual, calculated overall and then used as a proportion
p_xpert_repeat
# At the pooled test level, cartridge use is estimated as follows: each pool is assumed to require one cartridge for the first Xpert run (P1), and an additional cartridge only if a repeat pooled test (P2) was performed—thus, cartridge_used = 2 when both P1 and P2 exist, otherwise 1. This approach assumes that no pool undergoes more than one repeat (i.e., a maximum of two pooled tests per pool).
# 
# The total number of pooled cartridges (n_pool_cartridges) is calculated as the sum of observed cartridges used across all pools, capturing only the actual first and repeat runs.
# 
# To estimate the total number of cartridges required by the full pooling algorithm, the model adds the expected individual testing burden for positive pools where each MTB-positive pool triggers testing of all its individual specimens, inflated by the empirically observed proportion of Xpert repeats (xpert_repeat_prop).
```


```{r potential application}
# This can be done as a sensitivity analysis.
# by Country_Name + poolsize
summary_efficiency_cn <- summarize_pooling_efficiency(master_pool, master, p_xpert_repeat, group_var = "Country_Name", by_poolsize = TRUE)

# by Country_Name only (poolsize collapsed)
summary_efficiency_cn_total <- summarize_pooling_efficiency(master_pool, master, p_xpert_repeat, group_var = "Country_Name", by_poolsize = FALSE)
```
